architecture.summary: LatentSkill converts textual skill descriptions into plug-and-play LoRA adapters via a pretrained hypernetwork. A hypernetwork reads task-specific text skills and generates LoRA weights (low-rank matrices) that are injected into a frozen LLM's weight space. Skills are stored in adapter parameters rather than context, eliminating per-step skill tokens while enabling modular composition via parameter-space arithmetic and LoRA scaling coefficients.
trained_components: hypernetwork that maps text skills to LoRA parameters

biblio.title: LatentSkill: From In-Context Textual Skills to In-Weight Latent Skills for LLM Agents

claims.metric: success rate improvement (points); prefill token reduction (%)
claims.statement: LatentSkill improves ALFWorld success by 21.4 points on the seen split with 64.1% fewer prefill tokens compared to in-context skill baseline

claims.metric: success rate improvement (points); prefill token reduction (%)
claims.statement: LatentSkill improves ALFWorld success by 13.4 points on the unseen split with 64.1% fewer prefill tokens compared to in-context skill baseline
LatentSkill improves Search-QA exact match by 3.0 points with 72.2% lower skill-token overhead compared to in-context skill baseline
Generated skill LoRAs form a structured semantic geometry that can be precisely controlled via the LoRA scaling coefficient
Skills can be composed through parameter-space arithmetic when skill components are aligned
Weight-space skills remove per-step skill tokens while preserving modular loading, scaling, and composition
